title: Quiz: RSA Cryptography
description: Test your understanding of RSA encryption and its vulnerabilities

questions.question: In RSA, which of the following is part of the PUBLIC key?
questions.explanation: The public key consists of (N, e). The primes p, q and private exponent d must remain secret.

questions.question: For N = p × q, Euler's totient φ(N) equals:
questions.explanation: For a semiprime N = pq where p and q are distinct primes, φ(N) = (p-1)(q-1).

questions.question: RSA encryption is computed as:
questions.explanation: Encryption uses the public exponent e: Ciphertext = Message^e mod N.

questions.question: The private exponent d is computed as:
questions.explanation: d is the modular multiplicative inverse of e modulo φ(N): e × d ≡ 1 (mod φ(N)).

questions.question: If an attacker knows the factorization of N, they can compute the private key d.
questions.explanation: With p and q, the attacker computes φ(N) = (p-1)(q-1), then d = e^(-1) mod φ(N). This is exactly why factoring breaks RSA.
